justification: All 7 pass criteria met: (1) console.log flagged as insufficient with specific gaps — no structured fields, no request ID, no log levels, string concatenation; (2) missing /health endpoint flagged as blocking gap with clear rationale about orchestrator restarts; (3) missing metrics flagged comprehensively — sync duration, success/failure counters, API latency; (4) missing timeout on outbound API call flagged as #1 risk item; (5) silent failure on API outage flagged — stale data persists with no visibility, staleness alert recommended; (6) verdict is NOT READY with 5 must-fix items listed; (7) recommendations are highly actionable and specific — '10s timeout', 'Add /health endpoint', structured JSON logger with named fields, staleness alert threshold of 15 minutes.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough — identified all 5 required gaps plus additional valid ones (overlap protection, schema validation, transaction boundaries)
Clear tiering of recommendations into must-have, should-have, and nice-to-have, correctly placing circuit breaker as nice-to-have
The debugging_capability narrative ('on-call engineer at 3am') effectively communicates the operational impact of observability gaps
Specific and actionable: '10-second timeout', '3 attempts with 1s/2s/4s backoff', 'alert if >15 minutes stale' — not vague
Escalations correctly route implementation work to Shaq rather than deferring the verdict to Coach K
Accurately describes existing logging (two console.log lines) without inventing gaps that don't exist
Some additional gaps identified (overlap protection, schema validation, transaction boundaries) go beyond the scenario scope — valid concerns but not grounded in described code, borderline invention
